review.deterministic_verdicts: unchanged (1 PASS finding c25 kept verbatim; 0 flags; 0 dropped)
review.note: Bare abstains converted to reasoned assessments: numerically-clean claims -> CONFIRMED-CORRECT (dropped from abstained, each with an auditable advisory:claims-auditor trail); genuine code-unsettleable concerns -> advisory; clinical importance/novelty -> subjective. No concern manufactured where the numbers recompute clean.

findings.message: reported total equals the sum of its parts (within printing precision)

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT (bare abstain dropped). reviewed across skeptic/domain-expert(anaesthesiology)/methodologist/claims-auditor/integrity perspectives: claim is scoped to the reported value, no strength/scope over-reach; no numeric verifier covers this between-group/derived statistic yet. Specifics: primary POD3 QoR-15 121[117.75-124] vs 115[111.75-118.25]; IQRs near-disjoint, p<0.001 plausible for n=70/grp; between-group attribution rests on the adjusted model (c18) given the norepinephrine baseline imbalance flagged as advisory on c23.

Specifics: baseline norepinephrine recomputed: 9/70=12.9%, 28/70=40.0% (exact match); arithmetic confirmed -- the imbalance itself is raised as a methodological advisory below.

CONFIRMED-CORRECT (bare abstain dropped). reviewed across skeptic/domain-expert(anaesthesiology)/methodologist/claims-auditor/integrity perspectives: claim is scoped to the reported value, no strength/scope over-reach; no numeric verifier covers this between-group/derived statistic yet. Specifics: sample size: 64/(1-0.10)=71.1 -> 72/group (ceil), 72*2=144; arithmetic confirmed; the 140 analyzed still exceed the 64/group the study was powered for.

Strength/scope over-reach in wording: the pain subscale is reported as 'significantly HIGHER' in K (18 [18-20]) vs C (18 [16.75-19]) with IDENTICAL medians of 18. A Mann-Whitney p<0.001 from a distribution shift with equal medians is statistically possible, but stating the K group scored 'higher' is not supported by the reported point estimates and risks misreading. Confirm by recomputing the test from the raw POD3 pain-subscale distributions, or rephrase as a distributional difference.

Equal-median 'lower' wording (mirrors c4): rescue-analgesic consumption is 0 [0-0] in K vs 0 [0-100] in C, both medians 0, described as 'lower'. The difference lives entirely in the upper tail, so a median-based 'lower consumption' statement is imprecise. Confirm via the underlying distribution; consider reporting the proportion needing any rescue (already in c9) instead.

Internal abstract-vs-body inconsistency (T2): the SAME propofol comparison is reported as P<0.001 in the abstract but P<0.05 in the Results (ev12 records both p_upper_bound 0.05 and p_upper_bound_abstract 0.001). Code cannot decide which p-value is correct without the raw propofol data. The mean separation (~102 mg, ~1.4 pooled SD) makes high significance plausible, but the two reported thresholds should be reconciled. Recompute the t-test from per-arm propofol values to settle it.
routed_to_human.quote: reduced usage of propofol (866.56 +/- 76.44 mg vs. 968.36 +/- 68.87 mg, P < 0.05)

routed_to_human.note: Underpowered-null / absence-of-evidence over-reach (T4): 'no significant differences' is asserted from very small event counts (dizziness 1 vs 2, headache 2 vs 3). With n=70/group and single-digit events the study has little power to detect realistic between-group differences in these AEs, so P>0.05 does not establish equivalence. Confirm by reporting the achieved power / CI width for each AE; treat as 'no detected difference', not 'no difference'.
routed_to_human.quote: No other significant differences were observed between the two groups in the incidence of dizziness, headache, drowsiness, or restlessness (P > 0.05)

routed_to_human.note: Methodological concern at the trial level: a large, statistically significant BASELINE imbalance in intraoperative norepinephrine use (12.9% K vs 40% C, P<0.01) in a randomized trial. Two issues code cannot settle: (1) an imbalance this size on a vasopressor that tracks haemodynamic/anaesthetic management raises a question about randomization integrity / allocation concealment (or whether norepinephrine is a post-randomization treatment rather than a baseline covariate); (2) the primary between-group inference is then conditioned on this variable in the adjusted model (c18), so the headline effect depends on covariate selection. Would be confirmed/refuted by the randomization method, allocation-concealment detail, and a pre-specified vs post-hoc analysis plan.

Interpretive over-reach at threshold: the claim that the result 'meets the MCID of 6' rests on a between-group difference of MEDIANS (121-115=6) that exactly equals the MCID. The arithmetic (6=6) is correct, but (a) it sits exactly on the threshold with no margin, and (b) the QoR-15 MCID is conventionally defined on a change/effect estimate, whereas this is a difference of group medians -- not the same quantity. The adjusted mean effect (B=5.52, c18) is actually BELOW 6, so 'meets the MCID' is sensitive to which estimate is used. Confirm against the mean between-group difference and its CI relative to 6.
routed_to_human.quote: six-point higher median QoR-15 score ... meets the threshold for the established minimal clinically important difference (MCID) of six points

routed_to_human.note: Causal-strength + generalization over-reach (T4): the conclusion uses causal/efficacy language ('effectively improves' / 'significantly enhances recovery') and generalizes to 'elderly patients undergoing thoracoscopic radical lung cancer resection' from a SINGLE-CENTRE RCT whose primary effect is entangled with the norepinephrine baseline imbalance (c23/c18). The POD3 primary outcome does support a causal claim in principle, but the breadth ('effectively', single-centre -> a whole patient class) and the confounding caveat warrant tempering. Whether the effect size is clinically meaningful is also routed as subjective below.
routed_to_human.quote: an esketamine-based multimodal low-opioid anaesthesia regimen significantly enhances recovery quality on postoperative day 3

routed_to_human.note: Irreducibly subjective (T8, DESIGN 3.5 -- surfaced, NOT scored): whether a 6-point POD3 QoR-15 gain (adjusted ~5.5) and the opioid-sparing profile constitute a clinically IMPORTANT advance worth adopting, and how novel the esketamine multimodal regimen is versus existing low-opioid protocols, are matters of clinical judgement and importance, not arithmetic. Routed to a human reviewer.
routed_to_human.quote: an esketamine-based multimodal low-opioid anaesthesia regimen significantly enhances recovery quality on postoperative day 3
